children.text: Submarginal lands of United States held in trust for specified Indian tribes

Except as hereinafter provided, all of the right, title, and interest of the United States of America in all of the land, and the improvements now thereon, that was acquired under title II of the National Industrial Recovery Act of

children.tail: ), the Emergency Relief Appropriation Act of

children.text: section 55 of the Act of August 24, 1935

children.tail: , 781), and that are now administered by the Secretary of the Interior for the use or benefit of the Indian tribes identified in

, together with all minerals underlying any such land whether acquired pursuant to such Acts or otherwise owned by the United States, are hereby declared to be held by the United States in trust for each of said tribes, and (except in the case of the Cherokee Nation) shall be a part of the reservations heretofore established for each of said tribes.

children.text: Imposition of conditions on conveyed lands; lands excepted from conveying authority

The property conveyed by this chapter shall be subject to the appropriation or disposition of any of the lands, or interests therein, within the Pine Ridge Indian Reservation, South Dakota, as authorized by sections 441j to 441

children.tail: of title 16, and subject to a reservation in the United States of a right to prohibit or restrict improvements or structures on, and to continuously or intermittently inundate or otherwise use, lands in sections 25 and 26, township 48 north, range 3 west, at Odanah, Wisconsin, in connection with the Bad River flood control project as authorized by

children.text: section 203 of the Act of July 3, 1958

children.tail: , That this chapter shall not convey the title to any part of the lands or any interest therein that prior to
, have been included in the authorized water resources development projects in the Missouri River Basin as authorized by

children.text: section 203 of the Act of July 3, 1958

children.tail: , That such lands included in Missouri River Basin projects shall be treated as former trust lands are treated.

children.text: Additional lands held in trust for specified Indian tribes

The right, title, and interest of the United States of America in all of the lands, including the improvements now thereon (title to which is in the United States), acquired under title II of the National Industrial Recovery Act of

children.tail: ), and any subsequent Emergency Relief Appropriation Acts, including but not limited to section 5 of the Emergency Relief Appropriation Act of 1939 (
, 930) and section 4 of the Emergency Relief Appropriation Act, fiscal year 1941 (

, 617), together with all minerals underlying any such land whether acquired pursuant to such Acts or otherwise owned by the United States, and which lands are now administered by the Secretary of the Interior for the use or benefit of (1) Ramah Navajo Indians, are hereby declared to be held in trust for the Ramah Band of the Navajo Tribe, and (2) Choctaw Indians of Mississippi, except lands subject to the Act of
), are hereby declared to be held in trust for the Mississippi Band of Choctaw Indians; excepting valid rights-of-way of record.
